title: [MooreToCore][Sim] Implementation of queues in Sim
body:
This PR adds a new datatype to represent queues in the Sim dialect, as well as the following key operations:
- `QueueEmptyOp` to create an empty queue.
- `QueueSizeOp` to get the length of a queue.
- `QueuePush{Front/Back}Op` creates a new queue with an element added to the beginning/end.
- `QueuePop{Front/Back}Op` creates a new queue with one element removed from the beginning/end, returning the new queue and this element.

These are all pure operations, the idea being that later passes can avoid re-creating queues if not necessary.

The impure Moore ops for queues are lowered using the following strategy:
- Determine the source operation for the `queue` argument.
- Assuming this is a `ProbeOp`, note the signal being probed.
- Emit a `DriveOp` which writes the updated queue back to the signal.

Discussion point: When is the assumption that a queue comes from a `ProbeOp` likely to be violated? One example I can think of is when returning a queue from a function call. In this case, though, the effect on the queue is impossible to observe. (Since queues are passed by value, and the the push/pop ops in Moore don't return anything.)

If @tobiasgrosser @Scheremo could perhaps review when available :)
